name: David Sacks
text: So what caused the rapid rate hikes? The worst inflation in 40 years. And what caused that? Profligate spending and money printing coming out of Washington—all while Biden, Yellen and Powell assured us inflation was “transitory.”

name: Ted Lieu
text: The dramatic rise in GameStop stock price raises the question to me of whether there is any societal value to shorting stock? I’m not sure there is. In which case my initial reaction is that it’s awesome Reddit users and others are sticking it to hedge funds. Do you agree?

name: CNBC
text: "What's happening with GameStop is a pushback against the establishment in a really important way," @chamath says. Anyone who is dismissive should read the forums, he adds. https://t.co/Tlk0Zy5hFa https://t.co/39jxvGhdyY

name: Elon Musk
text:
Gamestonk!! 
https://t.co/RZtkDzAewJ

name: Ted Cruz
text: Fully agree. 👇

name: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez
text:
This is unacceptable.

We now need to know more about @RobinhoodApp’s decision to block retail investors from purchasing stock while hedge funds are freely able to trade the stock as they see fit.

As a member of the Financial Services Cmte, I’d support a hearing if necessary.

name: Rashida Tlaib
text: This is beyond absurd. @FSCDems need to have a hearing on Robinhood's market manipulation. They're blocking the ability to trade to protect Wall St. hedge funds, stealing millions of dollars from their users to protect people who've used the stock market as a casino for decades.

name: Motherboard
text:
More than half of all Robinhood users own at least some GameStop stock.

They are now unable to freely trade it; the app is only allowing users to close out their positions. https://t.co/DgN1H496wx

name: Sam Altman
text: fuck robinhood

name: Donald Trump Jr.
text: It took less than a day for big tech, big government and the corporate media to spring into action and begin colluding to protect their hedge fund buddies on Wall Street. This is what a rigged system looks like, folks! #RobinHood #RedditArmy #GME #GMEtothemoon

name: Dave Portnoy
text: Somebody is going to have to explain to me in what world @RobinhoodApp and others literally trying to force a crash by closing the open market is fair?   They should all be in jail.

name: Mark Cuban
text: I got to say I LOVE LOVE  what is going on with #wallstreetbets. All of those years of High Frequency Traders front running retail traders,now speed and density of information and retail trading is giving the little guy an edge. Even my 11 yr old traded w them and made $

name: Chamath Palihapitiya
text:
I remember when I met the @RobinhoodApp founders when they were raising their Seed, Series A and Series B.  I passed.

Why? 

Optimize for integrity whenever possible because integrity compounds and assholes will fuck you.

#DeleteRobinhood

name: Byron Donalds
text:
Private investors deserve to know Wall Street and hedge funds are not engaged in malicious behavior when the pendulum swings in favor of private investors, not corporate cronies. #RobinHood #WallStreet 

My full statement below: https://t.co/PkyepB4Ac4

name: Ben Shapiro
text: Note: Robinhood absolutely should not be stopping trading of GameStop. Market mechanisms include the reality that some people will attempt to game the market mechanisms -- and some will pay the price. Restricting who can buy and sell is using a hammer on free markets.

name: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez
text: Gotta admit it’s really something to see Wall Streeters with a long history of treating our economy as a casino complain about a message board of posters also treating the market as a casino

name: Julie
text:
Tools aren't going to make you great designers. 

Your way of thinking, attention to detail, and ability to see the bigger picture will.

name: Monica Lent
text:
When I worked at a startup, I used to hate it when Sales promised features the dev team had no time to deliver.

Now that I'm a bootstrapped founder I have no one to hate but myself 🙃

name: Dagobert - Corporate sellout 👔

text:
The only 3 skills you need to be a successful founder are:

1. Knowing what product to make
2. Knowing how to build it
3. Knowing how to sell it

name: Dan Rowden
text:
2020 mini review:

Was supposed to start a T-shirt company. 

Ended up launching 2x SaaS, a theme business, a productised service and a host. And I went deep into Ghost.

My most productive and successful year yet.

name: Brianne Kimmel
text:
Common misconception: joining a startup = fewer mentors & less exposure to executives  

In reality, the entire ecosystem opens up to you — investors, peers at other companies + execs at later stage startups want to help

It’s building together vs. learning by sitting in meetings

name: Siqi Chen
text: Every startup says they want an exceptional team but most don’t actually want it enough to pay what exceptional people are worth.

name: Adam Draper ⏻

text:
The greatest moment of my startup  was on the day we sold, when I reached out to two of our earliest investors who asked for their money back:
I sent them a breakdown of how much money they would’ve made.

name: Zach Vorhies / Google Whistleblower
text:
Update:

Crowdstrike came out and released a technical report confirming my analysis. They were reading in a bad data file and attempting to access invalid memory.

This global crash was a two-part bomb. The detonator apparently, was NOT new.. it was PRE-INSTALLED.

/1

name: Zach Vorhies / Google Whistleblower
text:
Crowdstrike Analysis:

It was a NULL pointer from the memory unsafe C++ language.

Since I am a professional C++ programmer, let me decode this stack trace dump for you. https://t.co/uUkXB2A8rm

name: Justin Fineberg
text:
The GPT playground didn’t go viral, chatGPT did. 

The UX is what allowed the masses to understand the power of AI. 

Goes to show that even when the underlying technology is the same, the best AI products will be the ones that are overly-focused on the user experience.

name: Brad Neuberg
text: ChatGPT is incredibly powerful as a tutor. I used it this morning for it to teach me more about Kalman filters, had it explain the equations to me, had it cast it into Python & Numpy since I learn better from code.

name: Bill Lennon
text: There’s a future where OpenAI is the only game in town for LLMs. It’s clear that an enormous amount of data and human feedback is needed to get current models to perform like ChatGPT which is head and shoulders beyond any competitor.

name: Lior Alexander
text:
ChatGPT is taking over the internet. But do you know how it actually works? It's so clever.

🧵Here's an explanation using simple words:

name: Molly White
text:
i just saw a tweet about someone using ChatGPT to write a smart contract

this is going to be a busy year for me, isn't it

name: Riley Goodside
text: I’m done with ChatGPT for a while. It’s sucked the joy out of prompt writing for me. Default writing quality is easy to achieve but hard to improve; opaque front-end and high temperature prevent the sort of casual analysis and experimentation that made fall in love with GPT-3.

name: Eliezer Yudkowsky ⏹️
text: Pouring some cold water on the latest wave of AI hype:  I could be wrong, but my guess is that we do *not* get AGI just by scaling ChatGPT, and that it takes *surprisingly* long from here.  Parents conceiving today may have a fair chance of their child living to see kindergarten.

name: Alexandr Wang
text:
The one thing we know works for education is 1-1 tutoring vs one-to-many classroom

1-1 tutoring with humans for everyone is economically infeasible

But the promise of AI and ChatGPT systems will be 1-1 tutoring for everyone in the world

Future is bright :)

name: Alex Cohen
text:
I mean this sincerely when I say that ChatGPT might be the most incredible tech to emerge in the last decade.

Here's how I got it to create a weight loss plan, complete with calorie targets, meal plans, a grocery list, and workout plan 🧵:

name: James Campbell
text:
Think about the difference between the fanfare raised by the GPT-4 release and that of GPT-3. Then compare to GPT-2 (and GPT-1 for that matter).

AI is going mainstream, poised to take over the world.

I expect GPT-6 to be unveiled by the President on live television.

name: rohit
text: This might be heresy, but the delta from GPT2 to GPT3 seems much wider than GPT3 to GPT4. It's getting more usable, but I'm not sure if the exponential improvement in capability hypothesis holds true any longer.

name: Travis Fischer
text:
Yesterday's GPT-4 launch was **crazy** 🔥

After discussing w/ colleagues and taking some time to collect my thoughts,

Here are the top 3 things GPT-4 enables that were previously impossible or extremely difficult to achieve – including examples 👇

name: Lior Alexander
text:
OpenAI truly built a world-class engineering team.

The last 8 months:

Aug '22 → DALL·E
Nov '22 → ChatGPT
Sep'22  → Whisper
Dec '22 → Point·E
Feb '23 → ChatGPT Plus
March '23 → ChatGPT API
March '23 → GPT-4

name: Yannic Kilcher 🇸🇨
text: GPT-4 paper literally is just saying "we trained a model on data and it's better". Spread over 98 pages.

name: Smoke-away
text: ❗BREAKING: OpenAI has released GPT-4.

text:
GPT 4, Claude, Alpaca, ChatGLM, PALM API...
